message:
fix(shadow): serialize env-var-dependent tests with ENV_LOCK mutex (bd-js27)

Shadow tests that manipulate PROCESS_TRIAGE_DATA env var were racing
when run in parallel, causing flaky failures. Added a static Mutex
guard to all 9 affected tests to serialize env var access.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-dg03","title":"E2E: TUI workflows with PTY recording + JSONL logs","description":"## Goal
Provide end-to-end TUI test scripts with PTY capture and detailed JSONL logging.

## Scope / Subtasks
- PTY harness to drive TUI navigation and keybindings deterministically.
- Record terminal output (asciinema/script) with timestamps.
- JSONL logs: inputs, focus changes, selected actions, exit codes, render timings.
- Validate key TUI flows: open, search/filter, drilldown, toggle actions, apply/abort, exit.
- Flake control: stable terminal size, deterministic seed, timeout watchdogs.

## Logging & Artifacts
- JSONL schema fields: event, timestamp, key, widget, action, exit_code, duration_ms, artifacts[].
- PTY recordings (asciinema) and screenshots per major screen.
- Artifact manifest includes recordings + logs + summary.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] TUI E2E runs deterministically in CI.
- [ ] Logs and screen recordings stored as artifacts.
- [ ] Flows cover apply/abort and drilldown navigation.
- [ ] JSONL schema validation passes.

## Test Plan
- E2E: scripted PTY run with recorded artifacts.
- Logging: JSONL schema validation + artifact manifest validation.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:05:21.458125743Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-02T19:59:18.474955263Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-dg03","depends_on_id":"bd-jg2d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T19:59:18.468590124Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-dg03","depends_on_id":"process_triage-2ka.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:05:21.482007393Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-dg03","depends_on_id":"process_triage-aii.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:05:21.467150668Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-dg03","depends_on_id":"process_triage-aii.7.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:22:29.171194703Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-dg03","depends_on_id":"process_triage-aii.7.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:05:21.474610544Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-hwvl","title":"No-mock session/plan/verify/resume tests","description":"## Goal
Exercise session lifecycle, plan generation, apply/verify, and resumability **without mocks**.

## Scope / Subtasks
- Session creation, manifest/context persistence, and state transitions.
- Plan generation with real `DecisionBundle` fixtures.
- Apply/verify identity revalidation (TOCTOU protection) using real processes.
- Resume partially-applied plans and verify idempotency.
- Cross-check plan schema against agent contract outputs.
- PID reuse scenarios: boot_id mismatch, start_time mismatch, recycled PIDs.
- Snapshot artifact persistence integrity: hash generation, tamper detection, and serialization-failure paths (e.g. NaN) fail closed.

## No-mock Policy (Commentary)
- Use real filesystem sessions under temp dirs.
- Use real child processes for identity/TOCTOU tests.
- Do **not** mock session store, plan builder, pre-checks, or executor.

## Logging & Artifacts
- JSONL logs for state transitions, action_ids, verify outcomes, and resume status.
- Log schema fields: event, timestamp, session_id, phase, command, exit_code, duration_ms, artifacts[].
- Artifact manifests list session dirs + plan JSON + verify outputs for CI upload.

## E2E Coverage
- Add `test/session_lifecycle_e2e.bats`: plan -> apply -> resume -> verify with real processes.
- E2E uses runner harness (process_triage-aii.7.7) and emits JSONL logs using the shared schema.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Session lifecycle covers Created -> Planned -> Executing -> Completed paths.
- [ ] Identity revalidation fails closed on PID reuse and mismatch scenarios.
- [ ] Resumability is deterministic and idempotent.
- [ ] Schema validation for plan/verify outputs passes.
- [ ] Snapshot artifacts fail closed on serialization errors; integrity hash validated on load.
- [ ] JSONL logs emitted with stable schema.
- [ ] E2E lifecycle run validates outputs and artifacts.

## Test Plan
- Integration: spawn real processes; validate plan/apply/verify.
- E2E: plan -> apply -> resume -> verify (no mocks).
- Logging: JSONL schema validation + artifact manifest.","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CobaltBeaver","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:05:12.846956563Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T16:40:57.314941598Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-hwvl","depends_on_id":"bd-jg2d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T19:59:30.354821944Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-hwvl","depends_on_id":"process_triage-aii.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:05:12.859210660Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-hwvl","depends_on_id":"process_triage-aii.7.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:05:12.864241301Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-hwvl","depends_on_id":"process_triage-aii.7.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:22:23.666137713Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-hwvl","depends_on_id":"process_triage-aii.7.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T19:45:26.514790492Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-jg2d","title":"Define E2E artifact manifest spec + validator","description":"## Goal
Define a **shared E2E artifact manifest spec** and validation tooling so every test emits consistent, machine‑readable artifacts.

## Scope / Subtasks
- Define a versioned manifest schema (JSON) with required fields:
  - schema_version, run_id, suite, test_id, timestamp
  - env (os, arch, kernel, ci provider, capabilities)
  - commands[] (argv, exit_code, duration_ms)
  - logs[] (path, kind=jsonl/text, sha256, bytes)
  - artifacts[] (path, kind, sha256, bytes, redaction_profile)
  - metrics (timings, counts, flake_retries)
- Provide validator CLI utility (or test helper) that:
  - validates schema
  - verifies file existence and checksums
  - enforces redaction profile field when required
- Document manifest layout and include examples in `test/fixtures/manifest_examples/`.
- Add unit tests for schema validation and checksum verification.

## Logging & Artifacts
- Manifest itself is an artifact and must be hashed.
- JSONL logs must reference the manifest `run_id` for correlation.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Manifest schema is versioned and documented.
- [ ] Validator rejects missing/invalid fields and bad checksums.
- [ ] Examples exist for TUI, install, daemon, and bundle/report E2E runs.
- [ ] Unit tests cover schema evolution (backward compatible changes).

## Test Plan
- Unit: schema validation + checksum verification.
- Integration: generate a manifest for a sample run and validate it.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CobaltBeaver","created_at":"2026-02-02T19:59:01.907912841Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T08:41:56.265478358Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T08:41:56.265385806Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-jg2d","depends_on_id":"process_triage-aii.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T19:59:01.921060778Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-jg2d","depends_on_id":"process_triage-aii.7.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T19:59:09.679304299Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-jg2d","depends_on_id":"process_triage-f5o","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T00:02:29.324238179Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-kwif","title":"Add missing options to agent verify command","description":"## Task
Add missing options to `pt agent verify` command per CLI specification.

## Current Implementation
```
pt agent verify --session <id>
```

## Missing Options (per CLI_SPECIFICATION.md:372-384)
```
--wait <seconds>     Wait for outcomes to stabilize before checking
--check-respawn      Check for respawned processes (supervised restarts)
```

## Implementation

### AgentVerifyArgs struct changes
```rust
#[derive(Args, Debug)]
struct AgentVerifyArgs {
    /// Session ID (required)
    #[arg(long)]
    session: String,
    
    /// Wait for process termination with timeout (seconds, default: 0 = no wait)
    #[arg(long, default_value = "0")]
    wait: u64,
    
    /// Check for respawned processes after kill
    #[arg(long)]
    check_respawn: bool,
}
```

### --wait Behavior
1. Collect PIDs of processes marked for termination in plan
2. Sleep in 250ms intervals, checking /proc/<pid> existence
3. Stop when all PIDs gone OR timeout reached
4. Then proceed with normal verification

```rust
if args.wait > 0 {
    let wait_duration = Duration::from_secs(args.wait);
    let start = Instant::now();
    let target_pids: Vec<u32> = plan.candidates
        .iter()
        .filter(|c| c.recommended_action == "terminate" || c.recommended_action == "kill")
        .map(|c| c.pid)
        .collect();
    
    while start.elapsed() < wait_duration {
        let still_running: Vec<_> = target_pids.iter()
            .filter(|pid| Path::new(&format!("/proc/{}", pid)).exists())
            .collect();
        
        if still_running.is_empty() {
            break;
        }
        thread::sleep(Duration::from_millis(250));
    }
}
```

### --check-respawn Behavior

#### Respawn Detection Algorithm
Respawn detection uses **normalized command signatures** to avoid false positives:

```rust
/// Normalize command for respawn comparison
fn normalize_command(cmd: &str) -> String {
    // Extract base command (first word)
    let parts: Vec<&str> = cmd.split_whitespace().collect();
    let base = parts.first().map(|s| {
        // Remove path prefix
        s.rsplit(/).next().unwrap_or(s)
    }).unwrap_or("");
    
    // Include key arguments that identify the process
    // Exclude ephemeral arguments (port numbers, temp files, timestamps)
    let key_args: Vec<&str> = parts.iter().skip(1)
        .filter(|arg| {
            // Keep: test files, watch flags, config files
            !arg.starts_with("--port") &&
            !arg.contains("/tmp/") &&
            !arg.parse::<u32>().is_ok()  // Skip bare numbers
        })
        .take(3)  // Limit to first 3 significant args
        .copied()
        .collect();
    
    format!("{} {}", base, key_args.join(" ")).trim().to_string()
}

// Match respawned processes
if args.check_respawn {
    let killed_signatures: Vec<String> = plan.candidates
        .iter()
        .filter(|c| c.action_taken == Some("terminated".into()))
        .map(|c| normalize_command(&c.cmd_full))
        .collect();
    
    let current_processes = scan_processes();
    
    let respawned: Vec<_> = current_processes
        .iter()
        .filter(|p| {
            let sig = normalize_command(&p.cmd);
            killed_signatures.contains(&sig) && 
            p.start_time > plan.apply_time  // Started after we killed
        })
        .collect();
}
```

#### Supervisor Detection
For respawned processes, attempt to identify the supervisor:
```rust
fn identify_supervisor(ppid: u32) -> Option<String> {
    let parent_cmd = read_proc_comm(ppid)?;
    match parent_cmd.as_str() {
        "systemd" => Some("systemd".into()),
        "supervisord" => Some("supervisord".into()),
        "pm2" => Some("pm2".into()),
        "nodemon" => Some("nodemon".into()),
        "docker-init" => Some("docker".into()),
        _ => None
    }
}
```

### Enhanced Output
```json
{
  "session_id": "...",
  "verification": { ... },
  "action_outcomes": [ ... ],
  "respawn_check": {
    "enabled": true,
    "respawned_count": 2,
    "respawned_processes": [
      {
        "pid": 5678,
        "comm": "jest",
        "cmd": "jest --watch",
        "normalized_signature": "jest --watch",
        "started_at": "2026-01-22T14:05:00Z",
        "likely_supervisor": "systemd",
        "original_killed_pid": 1234
      }
    ],
    "warning": "2 processes may have respawned after termination",
    "recommendation": "Consider stopping the supervisor or using --exclude-supervised in future"
  }
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `--wait N` delays verification by up to N seconds
- [ ] `--wait` exits early if all targets gone (no unnecessary delay)
- [ ] `--check-respawn` detects respawned processes using normalized signatures
- [ ] Respawn detection doesnt produce false positives for unrelated processes
- [ ] JSON output includes respawn_check section when enabled
- [ ] Summary format mentions respawns if detected
- [ ] Help text documents both options clearly

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_verify_wait_default_is_zero() {
    // Default wait is 0 (no wait)
}

#[test]
fn test_verify_wait_exits_early_when_processes_gone() {
    // If PIDs gone before timeout, dont wait full duration
}

#[test]
fn test_normalize_command_extracts_base() {
    // "/usr/bin/node" -> "node"
    assert_eq!(normalize_command("/usr/bin/node app.js"), "node app.js");
}

#[test]
fn test_normalize_command_strips_ephemeral_args() {
    // Port numbers and temp files stripped
    assert_eq!(
        normalize_command("node server.js --port 3000"),
        "node server.js"
    );
}

#[test]
fn test_respawn_detection_requires_later_start_time() {
    // Process started before kill is not considered respawned
}

#[test]
fn test_respawn_detection_uses_normalized_match() {
    // Exact signature match required, not substring
}
```

### Integration Tests
```rust
#[test]
fn test_verify_with_wait_polls_proc() {
    // Verify /proc/<pid> is checked during wait
}

#[test]
fn test_verify_respawn_check_scans_current_processes() {
    // Respawn check performs process scan
}

#[test]
fn test_supervisor_identification() {
    // Known supervisors are identified
}
```

### E2E Test Script (test/e2e_verify_options.sh)
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# E2E test for agent verify --wait and --check-respawn options

set -euo pipefail
LOG="test/logs/e2e_verify_options_$(date +%Y%m%d_%H%M%S).log"
mkdir -p test/logs

log() { echo "[$(date +%H:%M:%S)] $*" | tee -a "$LOG"; }

log "=== E2E Test: verify options ==="

# Create a test session with some candidates
log "Creating test session..."
SESSION=$(pt-core agent plan --format json | jq -r .session_id)
log "Session: $SESSION"

# Test --wait flag timing
log "Testing --wait flag (timing)..."
START=$(date +%s%N)
pt-core agent verify --session "$SESSION" --wait 2 --format json > /dev/null 2>&1 || true
END=$(date +%s%N)
ELAPSED_MS=$(( (END - START) / 1000000 ))

if [ "$ELAPSED_MS" -ge 100 ]; then
    log "✓ --wait caused some delay (elapsed: ${ELAPSED_MS}ms)"
else
    log "✓ --wait exited early (no targets to wait for, elapsed: ${ELAPSED_MS}ms)"
fi

# Test --check-respawn flag structure
log "Testing --check-respawn flag..."
OUTPUT=$(pt-core agent verify --session "$SESSION" --check-respawn --format json 2>&1 || true)
if echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -e ".respawn_check.enabled == true" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    log "✓ respawn_check.enabled is true"
    
    # Check for expected fields
    if echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -e ".respawn_check.respawned_count != null" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
        COUNT=$(echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -r ".respawn_check.respawned_count")
        log "✓ respawned_count field present (value: $COUNT)"
    fi
else
    log "✗ respawn_check section missing or enabled != true"
    echo "$OUTPUT" >> "$LOG"
    exit 1
fi

# Test combined flags
log "Testing --wait + --check-respawn..."
OUTPUT=$(pt-core agent verify --session "$SESSION" --wait 1 --check-respawn --format json 2>&1 || true)
if echo "$OUTPUT" | jq -e ".respawn_check" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    log "✓ Combined flags work"
else
    log "✗ Combined flags failed"
    exit 1
fi

# Test summary format mentions respawns (or at least executes)
log "Testing summary format..."
SUMMARY=$(pt-core agent verify --session "$SESSION" --check-respawn --format summary 2>&1 || true)
log "Summary output received ($(echo "$SUMMARY" | wc -c) bytes)"
log "✓ Summary format executed"

# Test --wait with very short timeout
log "Testing --wait with short timeout..."
START=$(date +%s)
pt-core agent verify --session "$SESSION" --wait 1 --format json > /dev/null 2>&1 || true
END=$(date +%s)
ELAPSED=$((END - START))
if [ "$ELAPSED" -le 2 ]; then
    log "✓ Short --wait respected (${ELAPSED}s)"
else
    log "⚠ --wait took longer than expected (${ELAPSED}s)"
fi

log "=== E2E Test PASSED ==="

 {"id":"bd-m3zh","title":"No-mock config/priors/policy validation tests","description":"## Goal
Validate config resolution, priors/policy parsing, and schema validation **without mocks**.

## Scope / Subtasks
- XDG/env/CLI config resolution end-to-end (precedence matrix).
- Priors/policy schema validation with real files (valid + invalid).
- Preset loading and override precedence tests.
- Regression tests for common user mistakes (missing fields, bad types, invalid enums).
- Policy invariants: protected patterns, robot gates, thresholds, and defaults.

## No-mock Policy (Commentary)
- Use real file fixtures and actual config loader paths.
- No mocked filesystem or config structs.

## Logging & Artifacts
- JSONL logs include config snapshot path, schema version, and validation errors.
- Log schema fields: event, timestamp, case_id, command, exit_code, duration_ms, artifacts[].
- Preserve failing fixture + error output for CI artifacts.

## E2E Coverage
- Add `test/config_matrix_e2e.bats`: run CLI with env/XDG/CLI overrides and validate behavior.
- E2E uses runner harness (process_triage-aii.7.7) and emits JSONL logs using the shared schema.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Resolution order and precedence documented and tested.
- [ ] Validation errors include stable, actionable messages.
- [ ] Presets resolve deterministically with overrides.
- [ ] Policy invariants verified (protected patterns, thresholds, gates).
- [ ] JSONL logs emitted for validation runs.
- [ ] E2E config matrix run passes with logged artifacts.

## Test Plan
- Unit: schema/validation errors with fixtures.
- Integration: env + CLI + XDG resolution matrix.
- E2E: config matrix run with JSONL logging.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:05:14.654022544Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T20:04:39.192901202Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T20:04:39.192820492Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-m3zh","depends_on_id":"bd-jg2d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T19:59:33.400479909Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-m3zh","depends_on_id":"process_triage-aii.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:05:14.666282371Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-m3zh","depends_on_id":"process_triage-aii.7.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T18:22:25.516346056Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-m3zh","depends_on_id":"process_triage-aii.7.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-02T19:45:28.692951387Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-ns1s","title":"E2E: config preset list/show/diff/export/validate","description":"## Goal
Exercise config preset workflows end-to-end with detailed logs and failure-path coverage.

## Scope
- `pt config list-presets` + `show-preset`.
- `pt config diff-preset` and export to file.
- `pt config validate` with valid + invalid configs.
- Failure paths: missing config file, invalid JSON schema, unsupported preset.
- Validate exit codes and user-facing error messages.

## Logging & Artifacts
- JSONL logs: event, cmd, exit_code, duration_ms, artifacts[].
- Artifacts: exported preset files, validation outputs, stderr snapshots.
- Log redaction checks (no secrets/path leakage).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All config commands succeed for valid presets.
- [ ] Invalid config paths fail with expected errors + correct exit codes.
- [ ] JSONL logs + artifacts produced for each command.

## Test Plan
- E2E script runs config commands against fixture configs.
